Game.Name: Virtual Soccer (Europe)
Game.plot: Overview:Virtual Soccer is a soccer game that uses a vertically-oriented perspective of the pitch with a slight Mode 7 angle. In Japan the game is known as J-League Super Soccer and features clubs from the national J.League, while the European version features international teams. Virtual Soccer was developed by Probe Entertainment, a UK company, and published by Hudson.The following international sides are represented in Virtual Soccer: England, Scotland, Ireland (referred to as Eire), France, Belgium, Holland (The Netherlands), Germany, Italy, Denmark, Sweden, Poland, Cameroon, Japan, Australia, New Zealand, USA, Canada, Colombia, Peru, Argentina, Uruguay, Brazil, Portugal and Spain.
Game.Notes: A soccer simulation game which takes place in your preference of side, top or 3/4 overhead view. The game dynamically zooms in and out at certain points of the game like a long goal kick. The mode of play are pre-season, custom teams, continental cup, nations shield, custom trophy and practice. Options to change the match length, weather and game speed are available.
